description: Domain vocabulary for the Zettle Finance API covering accounts, transactions, and payouts.

terms.definition: The type of a merchant's Zettle account. Either PRELIMINARY (unconfirmed transactions) or LIQUID (transactions ready for payout).
The account balance expressed in the currency's smallest unit (e.g. pence for GBP). Can be negative when refunds exceed sales.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code identifying the currency of an account (e.g. GBP, EUR, SEK).
The classification of a financial transaction in a Zettle account.
Zettle account type where transactions are pending confirmation before moving to the LIQUID account.
Zettle account type where confirmed transactions reside and are eligible for payout to the merchant's bank account or PayPal Wallet.
A transfer of funds from the merchant's LIQUID account to their registered bank account or PayPal Wallet. Negative amounts indicate an outgoing payout; positive amounts indicate a return of funds.
A card or alternative payment processed through Zettle where Zettle handles the funds. Refund results in an inverted-amount transaction of the same type.
The processing fee charged by Zettle for a PAYMENT transaction. Positive in the case of a refund.
Funds withheld from the LIQUID account to cover a chargeback initiated by the card issuer. Reversed with a positive transaction if the chargeback is revoked.
A cash advance provided by Zettle to the merchant based on their sales history, credited to the LIQUID account.
A retroactive adjustment to reduce a merchant's effective card processing fee rate, credited to the LIQUID account.
The frequency at which payouts are scheduled from the merchant's LIQUID account.
The projected amount to be transferred in the next scheduled payout, in the currency's smallest unit.
The remaining value of Zettle-issued marketing vouchers available to offset transaction fees, in the currency's smallest unit.
